headline: Southern’s 25 Year Dividend Growth Faces Valuation And Cash Flow Questions
summary: Southern Company (NYSE:SO) has increased its dividend for the 25th consecutive year. The latest dividend raise extends a quarter century of uninterrupted annual payout growth. This milestone underscores the utility's emphasis on consistent cash returns to shareholders. For investors watching income reliability, a 25 year streak of dividend increases at Southern Company stands out. At a share price of $91.87 and with a 5 year return of 72.1%, the company has paired income stability with long...
